{{Infobox character
|nom=Seven
|apparition=Appears in book 1
|status=Unknown
}}

== Biography ==

Seven is one of three gardeners tasked with repainting white roses red to conceal a planting error. He exhibits a sardonic manner, particularly regarding responsibility: when Five blames him for jogging his elbow and causing an accidental paint splash, Seven responds with, "That's right, Five! Always lay the blame on others!"—a remark that reveals his mocking approach to accountability. Like his companions, Seven appears anxious about the Queen's potential discovery of their deception. Upon noticing Alice observing their work, he abruptly ceases his protest ("Well, of all the unjust things—") and falls silent.

== Relationships ==

'''[[Alice]]''' — ally.

'''[[Five]]''' — acquaintance. Works alongside Five as a gardener painting the roses.

'''[[Queen]]''' — enemy. Fears the Queen's violent retribution.

'''[[Two]]''' — acquaintance. Works alongside Two as a gardener.

''Evolution:''
* [[Alice]] — Ally (ch.8)
* [[Five]] — Acquaintance (ch.8)
* [[Queen]] — Enemy (ch.8)
* [[Two]] — Acquaintance (ch.8)


[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Personnages du Tome 1]]
